title: Brian Brown, activistul american pro-life, prietenul politicienilor de AUR din România și al Rusiei
summary: The article investigates Brian Brown, president of the World Congress of Families, and his extensive ties to Russian-linked figures, oligarchs, and pro-Kremlin politicians, while documenting how Romania's AUR party has embraced Brown and his network. It traces the WCF's origins in Moscow, its connections to sanctioned Russian oligarch Konstantin Malofeev, and how these networks operate through pro-life and traditional family advocacy to advance Russian influence in Romania and Moldova.
talking_points: The World Congress of Families was founded in Moscow in 1995 and maintains deep institutional ties to Russian actors including sanctioned oligarch Konstantin Malofeev

Brian Brown has close relationships with multiple pro-Russian politicians including Moldova's former president Igor Dodon (exposed as a Russian intelligence collaborator) and Chișinău mayor Ion Ceban (banned from Romania for national security reasons)
AUR politicians have actively engaged with Brown's network, with George Simion announcing plans to host a World Congress of Families in Romania
Pro-life and traditional family advocacy organizations serve as vehicles for Russian influence operations in Romania and Moldova
Brown appeared alongside George Simion at the Romanian Parliament after the 2025 presidential election second round
related_concepts: Russian soft power and influence networks
author: Ileana Racheru
